reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures Axler Exercise 5.4. The setup `[AddCommGroup V] [Field F] [Module F V]` gives a vector space over a field. The hypothesis `S ∘ T = T ∘ S` (function composition equality for coerced linear maps) is equivalent to $ST = TS$ since linear maps are determined by their underlying functions. `ker (T - c • LinearMap.id)` correctly encodes $\text{null}(T - \lambda I)$, and the free variable `c : F` provides the universal quantification over $\lambda$. The conclusion `Submodule.map S (ker ...) ≤ ker ...` states $S(\text{null}(T - \lambda I)) \subseteq \text{null}(T - \lambda I)$, which is exactly $S$-invariance. No finite-dimensionality is needed for this theorem, so omitting it is correct.
